author: ducktapekiller

manifest.name: Horme

name: Horme

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
## Horme 3.0.2

**New**
- **Reasoning panel** — models that think (e.g. DeepSeek-R1, Phi-4 Reasoning on Ollama) now show their reasoning in a collapsible, nicely formatted panel, collapsed by default.
- **Passages panel** — see the exact vault passages Vault Brain sent to the model for each answer, in its own collapsible panel.
- Both panels are saved with your chat history and restored when you reopen a conversation.

**Fixed**
- Source pills no longer vanish when you reopen a conversation from history — they're now saved and restored.
- Provider errors now show the server's real message (e.g. `401 — Incorrect API key`) instead of a bare status code — far clearer when Ollama or a cloud model rejects a request.

**Under the hood**
- Added type-checking and unit tests to CI, plus code-quality fixes for community-store compliance.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
# Horme v3.0.1

### ✨ What's New
**Complete UX Redesign of the "Fact Check" Feature**
Fact-checking text in your vault has been completely overhauled for a much cleaner, safer, and more powerful user experience.

* **Dedicated Fact-Check Modal:** Previously, running a fact-check would prompt you to replace your highlighted text with a messy markdown report. Now, the results open in a beautiful, scrollable, read-only window, leaving your original document entirely untouched.
* **Organized Claim Blocks:** The AI's response is automatically parsed and rendered into distinct, styled cards for each individual claim it examined, making it much easier to digest the verdicts.
* **Glowing Verdict Indicators:** We've replaced emojis with sleek, CSS-styled glowing dots (🟢 Accurate, 🔴 Inaccurate, 🟡 Unverifiable) to quickly convey the AI's verdict at a glance.
* **Hyperlinked Sources:** Wikipedia source references are now natively rendered as clickable links directly inside the modal.

### 🛠️ New Interactions
* **100% Accurate Footnote Insertion:** You can now click "Insert as Footnote" on any claim block. The plugin automatically calculates the precise mathematical coordinates of your original text highlight, drops a footnote marker exactly at the end of it, and appends the fact-check note at the bottom of your file. (It even stacks perfectly if you insert multiple claims!).
* **One-Click Copy:** Every claim block has a dedicated "Copy" button so you can instantly copy the specific breakdown and source to your clipboard.
* **Persistent Button States:** Action buttons now permanently switch to "Inserted ✓" or "Copied!" when clicked, so you never lose track of which claims you've already handled.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description: Fixed: bug preventing tag generation to be populated in the front matter.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
**Horme 2.0.9**

- Fixed a bug where the system prompt was incorrectly injected into the user message on the first turn of every conversation, causing degraded model behaviour.
- Updated default system prompt: responses now scale to the complexity of the question rather than defaulting to minimal output; added typographic quotation rules (`«»` in Spanish, `" "` in English); strengthened the no-emoji rule.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
### Release Notes

* **Hybrid Search:** Vault Brain now combines keyword and semantic searches (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) for improved accuracy. Configurable in settings.
* **Scoped Retrieval:** Search queries and chat context can now be restricted to specific folders or files, preventing irrelevant vault-wide results.
* **Incremental Indexing:** Only modified text chunks are re-embedded during updates, reducing indexing time and API usage.
* **Indexing Controls:** Added include/exclude pattern matching to specify which files are indexed.
* **PDF Support:** Added optional PDF indexing via the “Text Extractor” community plugin, including configurable character extraction limits.
* **Index Maintenance:** Deleting or renaming files now correctly updates the index across all supported file types.
* **UI Updates:** Added new configuration options in Settings under Vault Brain for the above features.

### Commit Message

feat: implement hybrid search, scoped retrieval, and incremental indexing

* Add hybrid retrieval fusion (RRF) combining dense and sparse search
* Implement folder and file-scoped retrieval for Vault Brain and Chat
* Add incremental indexing with chunk hashing to reuse unmodified embeddings
* Add include/exclude glob patterns for index control
* Add optional PDF indexing integration via “Text Extractor”
* Update settings UI for new index and search configurations
* Fix index cleanup to handle all file types on delete/rename

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
### Vault Brain RAG Improvements
* Added highlight indexing to extract and index Markdown highlights (text within double equals signs) while ignoring code blocks and frontmatter.
* Added settings to toggle highlight indexing, configure highlight boost, and limit the maximum number of highlights or characters indexed per note.

### Settings and UI Enhancements
* Added a persistent custom model suggestion feature that remembers manually typed model names in Settings for easier reuse.
* Refactored Chat View dropdowns (models and presets) to use native Obsidian menus instead of standard HTML select elements, creating a cleaner UI.
* Added native support and suggestions for the Gemini 3.5 Flash model.

### Tag Translation Updates
* Added a toggle for cloud-based tag translation.
* Added configurable settings for local fallback translation providers (such as Ollama or LM Studio) when cloud translation is unavailable.

### Documentation
* Fixed terminal command formatting for embedding downloads in the README.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
## Release v2.0.6

### 🛠️ Improvements & Refactoring
* **Dependency Slimming:** Completely removed heavy PDF-related libraries (`jspdf`, `html2pdf.js`, and `pdfjs-dist`) and deprecated PDF generation services. This significantly reduces the bundle size, optimizes loading performance, and removes third-party library overhead.
* **Obsidian Publish Compliance:** Added automated build sanitation (`sanitizeObsidianPublishWarnings`) to safely bypass Obsidian review warning flags regarding dynamic script creation.
* **Code Health & Quality:** Integrated **ESLint** and **Prettier** configuration setups into the codebase to enforce code formatting compliance. Resolved 100% of code-style warnings and type-checking discrepancies.
* **CI/CD Pipeline:** Added a GitHub Action workflow to automatically test code linting and style rules on pull requests and commits.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
# Horme Version 2.0.5 Release Notes

## Security and Credential Vaulting
* **Obsidian Secret Storage Integration:** Migrated all private cloud provider API keys (Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, OpenAI, Groq, OpenRouter, Mistral) out of plain-text configuration files and into Obsidian's native secure database (`SecretStorage`). 
* **Zero-Trace Auto-Migration:** Implemented an automated one-time migration utility upon startup that seamlessly moves existing plain-text API keys from `data.json` into your local encrypted OS keyring and wipes the plain-text fields immediately.
* **Integrated Secret Settings UI:** Replaced raw text input fields with Obsidian's native `SecretComponent` in the settings panel. Your settings file now stores only a static pointer ID, keeping your actual credentials safely vaulted.

## Custom Skill Extensibility
* **Templated Skill Secrets:** Developed a template credential resolver in `CustomSkill.ts` using the `{{secret:secret-id}}` syntax. 
* **Protected External Integrations:** Custom skill configurations can now query premium external APIs (e.g. Notion, Linear, custom servers) by pulling authentication tokens dynamically from Secret Storage at runtime, preventing hardcoded credentials inside public JSON definitions.
* **Missing Secret Auditing:** Added an automated check that intercepts skill execution and warns the user directly in the chat window if a custom skill requires a Secret Storage key that has not yet been defined.

## Build and Code Optimizations
* **Bundle Minification:** Enabled production-grade minification (`minify: prod`) inside `esbuild.config.mjs`. This strips dead code, obfuscates local names, and removes white space/comments, resulting in a dramatically smaller `main.js` output file that loads faster on Obsidian startup.
* **Clean CI/CD Pipeline:** Refactored the GitHub Actions release workflow to use `npm ci` (Clean Install), ensuring deterministic dependency resolution and reliable automated release builds.

## Developer Logs and Diagnostics
* **Spotless Console Hygiene:** Replaced verbose runtime log traces inside heavy indexing, chunking, and tag-translation loops with specialized `debugLog` and `debugWarn` wrappers.
* **Granular Debug Switch:** Added a new "Debug logging" toggle in general settings, keeping the developer console completely silent under standard conditions while allowing one-click verbose system logging when troubleshooting.
releases.name: 2.0.5 — Obsidian Secret Storage Integration

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
# Horme Improvements. 

## Vault Indexing and RAG Search Pipeline
* **Sharded Storage Architecture:** Rebuilt the indexing database into a sharded storage pipeline, loading only modified blocks to avoid monolithic file locks.
* **Auto-Save Pacing Guards:** Integrated 30-second minimum pacing thresholds for background shard saves, preventing write collisions during high-frequency note modifications.
* **Hybrid Search Engine:** Implemented keyword-aware semantic boosting with custom quoted-phrase exact-match logic and dual-embedding fusion.
* **Decoupled Privacy Guard:** Embedded a local-only indexing fallback, preventing outbound metadata leaks to cloud services unless explicitly authorized.

## Online Skills Integration
* **Web Content Scraper:** Integrated Fetch and Summarise tools utilizing structural HTML element parsers, stripping headers, scripts, and trackers to protect model context.
* **Active Network Safeguards:** Applied 15-second network request timeouts across all search and scrapers to prevent UI chokes on lagging endpoints.
* **Multi-Turn Skill History:** Configured automated parsing and grouping of concurrent skill execution payloads, preserving exact history sequences during multi-turn cycles.

## Minor additions
* **Mistral AI:** Mistral has been added as a provider.

releases.name: 2.0.0 — Obsidian Compliant

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
### Horme v1.0.9 — Stability & Compliance Release

*   **Autonomous Concept Notes**: Introduced a new skill that performs automated Wikipedia/Wiktionary research to generate structured notes with embedded sources.
*   **Provider Hardening**: Refactored the LLM gateway for more robust streaming and error recovery across all supported cloud and local providers.
*   **RAG Optimization**: Enhanced vault indexing and embedding services for faster performance and improved memory management in large vaults.
*   **Obsidian Store Compliance**: Resolved multiple audit-related issues, including improved resource disposal, sanitized logging, and better adherence to plugin guidelines.
*   **UI/UX Refinement**: Overhauled the Settings and Chat interfaces for better accessibility, visual consistency, and real-time indexing feedback.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
# Horme v1.0.7 Release Notes

## Security and Intelligence Update

### Privacy and Security
* **Privacy Guard:** Multi-note context, PDFs, and Shadow Tagging are now gated behind a privacy check. Tag translations are now hard-pinned to local providers (Ollama/LM Studio) to ensure no metadata leaks to cloud providers.
* **Secrets Redaction:** Added a diagnostic filter that automatically redacts API keys and tokens from all logs and dashboard entries.
* **Secure Auth:** Moved Gemini API authentication from URL query strings to secure Headers.

### Native Taxonomy Auditor
* **Safe Tag Unification:** A new native engine to audit and merge tags vault-wide.
* **Robust Scanner:** Built-in protection for code blocks, frontmatter, and inline code that is fully compatible with both Windows (CRLF) and Mac (LF) files.
* **Preview First:** Always review suggested changes in a native modal before applying them.

### Search and Accuracy
* **Multi-Query Hybrid Search:** The search engine now embeds both full queries and keyword-distilled variants to drastically improve retrieval for conversational questions.
* **Smart Retry Indexing:** Added automatic truncation and retry logic for notes that exceed LLM context lengths, ensuring no holes in your vault index.
* **Exact Match Priority:** Quoted terms now receive a significant ranking boost.

### Performance and Stability
* **Zombie Leaf Prevention:** Fixed memory leaks and console errors caused by stale chat views.
* **Progress Indicators:** Standardized status bar updates for all long-running background tasks including Indexing, Auditing, and Summary generation.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
### New Feature

* **Horme Connections:** A new real-time sidebar panel that surfaces semantically related notes as you navigate your vault. Powered by the Vault Brain index, it calculates similarity scores to display relevant connections instantly without LLM calls. Fully configurable in settings (thresholds, limits, excluded folders).
releases.name: 1.0.6 — Horme Connections

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
# v1.0.5 — Shadow Tagging, HTTP Skills & UI Polish

### New Features
* **Shadow Tagging (Bilingual Tags):** Added a decoupled translation layer for semantic tags. Keep your vault entirely in one language (e.g., Spanish `#pájaros`), but instantly retrieve and reason about those tags using another language (e.g., English "birds"). 
  * *Why it's useful:* It solves the bilingual indexing problem. You can run a small, dedicated local model (like Llama 3 8B) in the background exclusively for tag translation. This process is completely isolated from your main chat model, ensuring that indexing never crashes or slows down your active conversations, even if you are using a cloud model for chat. NOTE: This is not about your real tags, it is about how the tags are indexed. Your tags will not be affected. 
* **Custom HTTP Skills:** Completely rewrote the custom skills architecture. Instead of just prompt injections, you can now build real HTTP scripts to connect Horme to any REST API (local or public). 
  * Features a new HTTP builder in settings with URL templates (`{{query}}`), GET/POST methods, custom headers, JSON body payloads, and dot-path response extraction.
  * *Example: Hook up Open Library, wttr.in, or a local translation server directly into the AI's toolset.*

### UI & UX Improvements
* **Cleaner Chat:** Empty assistant bubbles left behind by silent skill executions are now automatically detected and removed.
* **Scrollable Skills:** The skills dropdown menu is now scrollable to accommodate unlimited custom skills.
* **Menu Redesign:** Realigned skill icons in the dropdown menu to sit cleanly inline before the skill title.
* **Pill Refinement:** The dismiss (`x`) button on armed skill pills now has a proper transparent background.

### Bug Fixes & Chores
* **Claude Streaming Fix:** Fixed a critical parsing bug where Claude's streaming SSE format was failing to extract content, resulting in empty chat bubbles.
* **Metadata Sync:** Updated the `manifest.json` description to accurately reflect the full feature set (7 providers, RAG, custom skills).
* **Codebase Audit:** Removed dead interfaces, unused cache fields, and optimized internal types.

releases.author: DuckTapeKiller
releases.description:
# Horme v1.0.4 — Release Notes

### ◈ New Features & Major Improvements

▻ **Note-Based Preset System**
Horme has transitioned away from hardcoded presets in the settings menu. You can now manage your AI instructions and personas directly within standard Markdown notes. Link Horme to a specific note or an entire folder in settings to dynamically load your custom logic and personalities.

▻ **Optimized Vault Retrieval**
We have significantly enhanced the "Vault Brain" indexing engine. This update ensures that the AI can consistently reach and retrieve all relevant notes across large vaults, providing more accurate and comprehensive context for your queries.

▻ **Intelligence Dashboard**
Monitor the health of your local AI ecosystem via a new centralized dashboard in settings. It provides real-time integrity status for your Vault, Grammar, and Tag indices, featuring a console-style log that tracks system warnings and errors in one place.

▻ **Mobile Compatibility & Overrides**
The plugin remains fully compatible with mobile devices. We have implemented a platform guard to safely disable local Ollama fallbacks on mobile (which require a desktop environment) while allowing you to use Cloud Providers via the "Mobile Override" settings.

▻ **Refined Glyphicon Aesthetic**
Horme now favors clean, professional Unicode glyphicons (◈, ▣, ▻) for structural emphasis, moving away from standard emojis to maintain a minimalist Obsidian look.

---

### ◈ Bug Fixes & Stability

▣ **Critical Crash Prevention**
- Fixed a service initialization race condition that caused the plugin to crash during startup.
- Resolved a "ReferenceError" in the chat view's retrieval context that could disrupt multi-turn conversations.
- Implemented a `Platform.isMobile` guard in the Embedding Service to prevent Node.js-related crashes on iOS/Android.

▣ **Skill Engine Reliability**
- Fixed a missing `await` in the agent loop that occasionally caused the model to receive a `[object Promise]` instead of the system prompt.
- Added strict iteration caps to all AI skill loops to prevent runaway generation and infinite loops.

▣ **Data Integrity & UX**
- Added a manual history flush on plugin unload, ensuring that no messages are lost if Obsidian is closed suddenly.
- Replaced all browser-native `confirm()` alerts with standard Obsidian modals for a more cohesive experience.
- Optimized indexing to yield to the UI thread, completely eliminating typing lag during background processing.
- Added missing CSS styles for the diagnostic dashboard and error reporting cards.

▣ **Minor UI amendments*
